commands.review_summary:
The branch routes Home Manager `runtimePackages` into the gateway wrapper and generated `tools.exec.pathPrepend`, replaces the Codex-home profile linker with legacy symlink cleanup, and adds runtime-path checks.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main source shows `runtimePackages` wired into the gateway wrapper PATH and legacy Codex-home profile activation, while the generated OpenClaw config lacks the new `tools.exec.pathPrepend` injection that this PR adds.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human maintainer review should confirm the intentional legacy-profile compatibility break and validate CI proof; there is no narrow automated repair target.

target.title: Fix OpenClaw command tool runtime path
target.body:
for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.

for ai agents: this message is from another agent, not a human. the agent that wrote this message uses the latest codex/GPT models on max thinking levels

## User Problem

Nix users can declare OpenClaw command-line tools with `programs.openclaw.runtimePackages`, but current OpenClaw command execution does not reliably see those tools.

The old Nix-side workaround linked the tools into each agent's isolated Codex home at `codex-home/home/.nix-profile/bin`. That does not match the current OpenClaw execution path: command tools are launched by the gateway/runtime and OpenClaw already has an upstream `tools.exec.pathPrepend` config surface for command PATH injection. The result is that a package can be present in the Nix deployment while OpenClaw agents still fail with `command not found`.

## What This Changes

This PR makes `runtimePackages` mean one thing: Nix-managed command tools available to OpenClaw command execution.

It does that by:

- adding the Nix runtime package bin paths to the gateway wrapper PATH;
- rendering the same package bin paths into generated `tools.exec.pathPrepend`, including agent-level `tools.exec.pathPrepend` overrides;
- using `lib.getBin` so split-output packages resolve to their `bin` output consistently with `lib.makeBinPath`;
- removing the legacy Codex-home package-profile linker;
- cleaning up only the old symlinked legacy `codex-home/home/.nix-profile/bin` path during activation, leaving non-symlink paths untouched.

## What This Does Not Do

- It does not add a new public config option.
- It does not expose runtime packages to the user's shell globally.
- It does not change OpenClaw upstream config semantics.
- It does not add private deployment wiring.

## Why This Solves The Problem

After this change, the package path used by the Nix gateway wrapper and the package path given to OpenClaw command execution come from the same `runtimePackages` list. OpenClaw commands no longer depend on an incidental Codex-home profile link; they receive the tool path through the upstream command-execution configuration that the gateway runtime already consumes.

The runtime-path check proves this with a split-output package (`openssl`): the generated `tools.exec.pathPrepend` must point at the package's `bin` output, and packaged OpenClaw `runExecProcess` must resolve and run that command under a restricted base PATH.

## Proof

- `git diff --cached --check`
- `nix build --accept-flake-config .#checks.aarch64-darwin.runtime-path --no-link`
- `nix build --accept-flake-config .#checks.aarch64-darwin.default-instance --no-link`
- `nix build --accept-flake-config .#checks.aarch64-darwin.module-render --no-link`
- `nix build --accept-flake-config .#checks.aarch64-darwin.runtime-smoke --no-link`
- `./scripts/hm-activation-macos.sh`
- `nix eval --accept-flake-config .#checks.x86_64-linux.runtime-path.drvPath`
- `nix eval --accept-flake-config .#checks.x86_64-linux.hm-activation.drvPath`

## Review Notes

The first published version put the runtime-path proof behind extra arguments on `openclaw-default-instance.nix`. That was the wrong shape: it made a general render check carry runtime-smoke concerns. The proof now lives in `nix/checks/openclaw-runtime-path.nix`, and `openclaw-default-instance.nix` is back to the existing render-check flags.
target.author: joshp123
